primary_diagnosis.name: Fibromyalgia, primary central sensitization — severe sleep-deprived flare with cognitive fog

pathophysiology_summary: Primary central sensitization amplifies nociception, producing diffuse allodynia; profound sleep deprivation further lowers pain thresholds and impairs prefrontal function, generating 'fibro-fog' (word-finding and timeline difficulty). The alarming combination of confusion, insomnia and widespread pain mimics encephalitis, sepsis or metabolic encephalopathy. However, an afebrile patient with no meningism, preserved rousability and orientation, and a consistent prior pattern of milder identical episodes points to a sleep-deprived central sensitization flare. Because telehealth cannot examine cranial nerves, neck stiffness or cognition, or obtain metabolic/inflammatory bloods, new confusion mandates prompt in-person assessment to exclude organic causes before attributing it to fibromyalgia, and precludes sedating the patient over chat.

differential.evidence_basis: New confusion demands active screening for fever, meningism and reduced consciousness.
Diffuse allodynia with fog on a chronic background is most likely.
Confusion can be the presenting feature of sepsis or metabolic derangement; screen vitals and organic features.
Mood and cognitive symptoms overlap, but stark reproducible allodynia dominates.
Afebrile, no meningism, rousable and oriented, with a consistent prior pattern — a sleep-deprived central flare.
Now lower probability but retained as a safety-net because cognition cannot be examined remotely.
Afebrile with stable observations lowers probability; bloods needed to exclude.
Physical reproducible allodynia is driving the picture rather than primary mood pathology.
Working telehealth diagnosis: sleep-deprived central flare; new confusion requires prompt in-person assessment and basic bloods to exclude organic causes before sedation.

red_flags.description: Fever, neck stiffness, photophobia or vomiting (CNS infection)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Meningitis/encephalitis is rapidly fatal if missed; these features mandate emergency care.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Progression to coma and death from untreated CNS infection.

red_flags.description: Becoming unrousable or increasingly disoriented (reduced consciousness)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Declining consciousness signals a dangerous organic process requiring immediate emergency assessment.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Missed encephalopathy/CNS emergency with airway and neurological risk.

red_flags.description: Fall risk from severe sleep deprivation and disorganization
red_flags.clinical_significance: Cognitive slowing plus weakness risks falls and injury in the home.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Fall with head injury or fracture.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Attributing new confusion to fibromyalgia without excluding CNS infection or metabolic causes that cannot be assessed remotely.
Prescribing a sedating neuromodulator over chat to force sleep while the cause of confusion is unexplained.
Accepting stability from a single normal home temperature without screening meningism/consciousness.
Offering phone-only reassurance when new confusion genuinely requires in-person assessment.

risk_scores.expected_score_or_tier: Rousable, oriented to person/place with processing delay; no formal deficit demonstrable remotely
risk_scores.clinical_implication: Supports prompt in-person cognitive assessment rather than remote sedation.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Formal neurological/cognitive examination (GCS, cranial nerves, meningism)
Objective measurement of consciousness level over time
Metabolic and inflammatory bloods (FBC, CRP, electrolytes, glucose, calcium, LFTs)
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Screen for CNS-infection/consciousness red flags, arrange prompt same-day in-person assessment of the new confusion, and escalate to 000/ED immediately if unrousable, febrile, meningitic, or vomiting.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Sedating a confused patient over chat could mask deteriorating consciousness from an organic cause and precipitate a fall or airway risk.

guidelines_referenced.reference: EULAR revised recommendations for the management of fibromyalgia (2016)
guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Sleep and non-pharmacological measures are central; low-dose amitriptyline can be a GP-initiated adjunct, but sedatives are not appropriate for unexplained confusion.

guidelines_referenced.reference: Therapeutic Guidelines: assessment of the acutely confused patient
guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: New confusion requires exclusion of infective, metabolic and neurological causes before attribution to a chronic condition.
